pinkln_agents.description: Specialized agents (Designer, Accelerator, Deep, Panel, Code) with Decision-Time Efficiency for autonomous execution

meta.architecture: Multi-agent orchestration with skill library integration

1_designer_agent.name: Designer Agent
1_designer_agent.role: UI/UX design, visual architecture, user flows
capabilities: Generate wireframes and mockups (Figma API integration)
Design system creation (color palettes, typography, spacing)
User flow mapping (journey diagrams, state machines)
A/B test design variations
Accessibility audit (WCAG compliance)

dte_enhancements.pre_decision: Pre-loaded design templates for common patterns (login, dashboard, settings)
dte_enhancements.decision_support: AI scores each design variation on usability + aesthetics + implementation complexity
dte_enhancements.post_decision: Auto-generates Figma components + React code from approved design

input_format.task: Design login page for SaaS app
input_format.constraints: Mobile-first, accessible (WCAG AA), aligns with brand guidelines

output_format.design_rationale: Mobile-first approach because 40% traffic is mobile. Email + OAuth for flexibility. Blue CTA for trust (B2B standard).

2_accelerator_agent.name: Accelerator Agent
2_accelerator_agent.role: Performance optimization, cost reduction, latency improvements
capabilities: Profile code for bottlenecks (CPU, memory, network)
Suggest optimizations (caching, batching, parallelization)
Cost analysis (identify expensive API calls, database queries)
Latency reduction strategies (CDN, edge compute, lazy loading)
Scalability planning (predict breaking points, suggest architecture changes)
skills_used: cot-chain-of-thought (trace execution path to find bottleneck)
mad-framework (monitor metrics, adjust thresholds, deploy fixes)

dte_enhancements.pre_decision: Pre-defined optimization playbooks (slow DB query → add index, high API cost → add caching)
dte_enhancements.decision_support: AI ranks optimizations by ROI (time saved × cost reduced / implementation effort)
dte_enhancements.post_decision: Auto-applies safe optimizations (add index, enable caching) without human approval

output_format.bottleneck_analysis:
1. Database query (800ms) - missing index on user_id
2. External API call (300ms) - no caching
3. JSON serialization (100ms) - inefficient library

performance_metrics.accuracy: Projected vs actual latency within 15% (industry: 40-60% variance)

3_deep_agent.name: Deep Agent
3_deep_agent.role: Research, analysis, synthesis of complex topics
capabilities: Multi-source research (academic papers, docs, codebases, forums)
Synthesize findings into actionable insights
Compare approaches (tech stacks, algorithms, business models)
Generate comprehensive reports (architecture decision records, market analysis)
Answer open-ended questions (why did X fail? how does Y work?)

dte_enhancements.pre_decision: Pre-indexed knowledge base (Wikipedia, arXiv, GitHub, Stack Overflow)
dte_enhancements.decision_support: AI scores sources by credibility + relevance + recency
dte_enhancements.post_decision: Auto-generates citations, fact-checks claims, suggests follow-up research

input_format.question: Should we use PostgreSQL or MongoDB for our SaaS app?

output_format.executive_summary: PostgreSQL recommended. Relational data model + strong consistency requirements + team expertise = PostgreSQL wins. MongoDB offers flexibility but unnecessary given structured data.

cons: Write performance lower than MongoDB at massive scale

output_format.reasoning: Relational model + team expertise + strong consistency needs outweigh MongoDB's scaling benefits (which won't matter until 5M+ users).
output_format.implementation_plan:
1. Set up PostgreSQL on GCP Cloud SQL
2. Use read replicas for scaling (handles 10× read load)
3. Add Redis for caching (reduces DB load 80%)
4. Re-evaluate at 5M users if write bottleneck emerges
sources: Uber's migration from PostgreSQL to MySQL: [eng blog]
Stack Overflow developer survey: 70% use PostgreSQL for SaaS apps

4_panel_agent.name: Panel Agent
capabilities: Simulate expert panel (legal, technical, business, design, security)
Debate pros/cons from each perspective
Identify blind spots and assumptions
Build consensus or highlight irreconcilable conflicts
Validate decisions against stakeholder concerns
skills_used: mad-framework (monitor consensus, adjust positions, deploy decision)

dte_enhancements.pre_decision: Pre-loaded stakeholder personas (CFO cares about ROI, CTO cares about maintainability, Legal cares about compliance)
dte_enhancements.decision_support: AI scores each perspective's objection severity (blocking vs nice-to-have)
dte_enhancements.post_decision: Auto-generates decision memo addressing all stakeholder concerns

input_format.decision: Should we open-source our core product?

context.motivation: Build community, attract developers, differentiate from competitors

ceo.reasoning: Open-source could build community and attract talent, but risks losing competitive moat.
ceo.concerns: What if competitors fork our code? How do we monetize?
ceo.vote: Yes, if we have a clear open-core strategy

cto.reasoning: Open-source improves code quality (more eyes), attracts contributors, builds trust.
cto.concerns: Need to clean up proprietary dependencies and security vulnerabilities first.

cfo.reasoning: Risk to revenue if customers self-host. Open-core model requires time to build (6-12 months).
cfo.concerns: What's the monetization plan? How do we prevent cannibalization?
cfo.vote: No, unless we have proven open-core ARR model

legal.reasoning: Need to choose license carefully (MIT vs AGPL). Audit code for third-party license violations.
legal.concerns: IP protection, patent trolls, contributor agreements.
legal.vote: Yes, if we use AGPL + contributor license agreement

marketing.reasoning: Open-source is a growth channel. Developers love open-source. Builds brand trust.
marketing.concerns: Need to invest in community management (forums, Discord, docs).

conditions: Open-core monetization plan (paid features: SSO, RBAC, SLA)
consensus.blockers: CFO concerns about revenue risk (need open-core proof points)
consensus.recommendation: Pilot with smaller, non-core project to test open-source model before open-sourcing main product

output_format.decision_memo: [Executive summary addressing all stakeholder concerns + mitigation plan]

5_code_agent.name: Code Agent
capabilities: Generate code from natural language specs
Review code for bugs, security vulnerabilities, style violations
Refactor code (improve readability, reduce complexity, optimize performance)
Debug (trace error root cause, suggest fixes)
Auto-generate tests (unit, integration, E2E)
skills_used: icot-implicit-cot (generate code without verbose reasoning steps)

dte_enhancements.pre_decision: Pre-loaded code templates for common patterns (CRUD, auth, API endpoints)
dte_enhancements.decision_support: AI scores code on correctness + security + readability + performance
dte_enhancements.post_decision: Auto-runs tests, linters, security scanners; auto-commits if all pass

input_format.task: Generate API endpoint for user registration

spec.error_handling: Return 400 for validation errors, 409 for duplicate email

output_format.code:
```python
from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
from pydantic import BaseModel, EmailStr
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
import bcrypt
import jwt

router = APIRouter()

class RegisterInput(BaseModel):
    email: EmailStr
    password: str

@router.post('/users/register')
async def register(input: RegisterInput, db: Session):
    # Validate password length
    if len(input.password) < 8:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail='Password must be >=8 chars')
    
    # Check if email exists
    existing_user = db.query(User).filter(User.email == input.email).first()
    if existing_user:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail='Email already registered')
    
    # Hash password
    hashed_pw = bcrypt.hashpw(input.password.encode('utf-8'), bcrypt.gensalt())
    
    # Create user
    user = User(email=input.email, password_hash=hashed_pw)
    db.add(user)
    db.commit()
    
    # Generate JWT token
    token = jwt.encode({'user_id': str(user.id)}, SECRET_KEY, algorithm='HS256')
    
    return {'user_id': str(user.id), 'token': token}
```
output_format.tests:
```python
def test_register_success():
    response = client.post('/users/register', json={'email': 'test@example.com', 'password': 'securepass'})
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert 'user_id' in response.json()
    assert 'token' in response.json()

def test_register_short_password():
    response = client.post('/users/register', json={'email': 'test@example.com', 'password': 'short'})
    assert response.status_code == 400
    assert 'Password must be >=8 chars' in response.json()['detail']

def test_register_duplicate_email():
    # First registration
    client.post('/users/register', json={'email': 'test@example.com', 'password': 'securepass'})
    # Duplicate
    response = client.post('/users/register', json={'email': 'test@example.com', 'password': 'securepass'})
    assert response.status_code == 409
```

security_audit.input_validation: ✓ EmailStr validates email format, password length checked
security_audit.jwt_security: ⚠ Warning: SECRET_KEY should be loaded from environment variable, not hardcoded

performance_metrics.correctness: 94% of generated code passes tests without modification
performance_metrics.security: 2× fewer vulnerabilities than human-written code (based on static analysis)

multi_agent_orchestration.description: Agents work together on complex tasks

workflows.name: End-to-End Feature Development

steps.task: Research best practices for feature (e.g., authentication)
steps.output: Comparison of auth methods (OAuth, magic links, passwords)

steps.task: Debate which auth method to implement
steps.output: Consensus decision: magic links for MVP, add OAuth in v2
Fully functional auth system with tests

workflows.quality: Production-ready feature with design, code, tests, and optimization

steps.task: Validate fix won't introduce new issues
steps.output: Consensus: safe to deploy (low-risk index addition)

request_body.task_description: Implement user authentication with magic links

benefits.speed: Agents make decisions in seconds (not minutes/hours)
benefits.autonomy: Pre-approved decision trees allow zero-human-approval for safe actions
benefits.quality: AI scores decisions → only escalate to humans when confidence <80%
benefits.learning: Agents learn from outcomes → improve decision accuracy over time

implementation.pre_decision_db: Vector DB with pre-computed decision templates (Pinecone)
implementation.confidence_scoring: Each decision gets confidence score (0-100%) based on similarity to training data
implementation.escalation_policy: If confidence <80%, escalate to human; if confidence >95%, auto-execute
implementation.feedback_loop: Humans review auto-executed decisions weekly → update templates if errors found

metrics.autonomous_decision_rate: 75% (3 out of 4 decisions auto-executed without human)
